name: Justice
description_short: Justice Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving the justice, law enforcement, and public safety community since 1935.
description_long:
Justice Federal Credit Union has operated as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative since 1935, making it one of the longest-established credit unions in the United States. The institution serves a specific membership base: individuals in the justice, law enforcement, and public safety community across the nation, including active and retired federal employees through partnership with the National Active and Retired Federal Employees Association (NARFE).

Justice Federal offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products. Members can access checking and savings accounts (including high-yield savings earning 4.00% APY with direct deposit), share certificates with flexible terms (9, 13, or 36 months), personal loans up to $50,000 with terms to 84 months, auto loans with rates as low as 4.69% APR and terms up to 96 months, home equity lines of credit, and VISA credit cards with 0% introductory APR for 12 months. The credit union also provides digital banking through a mobile app and offers Ellie, a 24/7 virtual financial assistant for account management and transfers.

What distinguishes Justice Federal is its specialized membership targeting—eligibility requires serving or supporting the justice and law enforcement community, creating a niche focus unlike general-purpose banks. The institution emphasizes member ownership, with each share representing voting rights in annual board elections. They also offer member-specific programs such as special assistance during federal government shutdowns and exclusive partnerships like NARFE to provide tailored benefits to federal employees and retirees.

Justice Federal operates transparently as a credit union with clear rate disclosures and maintains active fraud prevention education resources. However, membership eligibility restrictions limit accessibility to the general public, and detailed information about fees, insurance products, and full account terms is limited in available website content. The credit union serves its niche market effectively but is not suitable for those outside the justice and public safety employment sectors.
services: High-yield savings accounts with direct deposit incentives
Personal loans up to $50,000 (terms to 84 months)
Auto loans and refinancing (terms to 96 months)
VISA credit cards with introductory 0% APR
Digital banking and mobile app access
Special assistance loans during federal government shutdowns
Fraud prevention and security education resources
pros: High-yield savings at 4.00% APY when direct deposit is added, competitive for savings products
0% introductory APR for 12 months on ALL credit card purchases, significantly above industry average for introductory offers
Auto loan terms up to 96 months with $0 down payment and up to 125% financing, providing flexibility for vehicle purchases
Member-owned cooperative structure means members are owners with voting rights on board elections
Specialized service to federal employees and public safety workers, including emergency assistance during government shutdowns
Personal loans up to $50,000 with extended terms to 84 months for debt consolidation
24/7 virtual financial assistant (Ellie) available for account management and transfers
cons: Membership is restricted to justice, law enforcement, and public safety community employees—not available to general public
Minimum $5 balance required to establish and maintain savings account membership, though modest
Limited transparency on website regarding monthly fees, overdraft charges, and full account terms
Website does not provide detailed information about insurance products, investment services, or business accounts
Auto loan deferred payment programs ($0 payment for up to 90 days) may encourage borrowing beyond means
best_for: Federal employees and contractors seeking specialized credit union services with emergency assistance options
Law enforcement and justice sector workers eligible for membership who want member-owned cooperative benefits
Federal retirees through NARFE partnership seeking competitive rates and member-focused services
Individuals in public safety careers needing vehicle financing or debt consolidation with flexible terms
diagnosis: Justice Federal Credit Union is best suited for federal employees, law enforcement, and public safety professionals seeking credit union benefits with competitive rates and member-owned structure. The main caveat is strict membership eligibility requirements that restrict access to those working in justice and public safety sectors, making it unsuitable for the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Justice — Credit Union in Chantilly, VA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Justice — NCUA-insured credit union in Chantilly, VA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
